title: luzula campestris var. luteola
intro: Luzula campestris var. luteola, also known as golden field woodrush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Juncaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It has been known to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Luzula campestris var. luteola is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
